description: Territories governed by religious or ecclesiastical authority.
regions: A broad swath of countryside and towns under a bishop's spiritual care and temporal sway.
A small circle of homes and fields tended by a single priest, centered on a humble chapel.
A cloistered enclave of prayer and scholarship, its walls enclosing gardens and quiet courtyards.
A great monastic house of vaulted stone, commanding tithes and farmland for leagues around.
The seat of a bishop's authority, radiating influence from a cathedral city across the surrounding land.
A vast ecclesiastical dominion guided by the eldest voice of the faith, spanning many lesser sees.
A distant territory granted special autonomy, governed by a trusted envoy of higher religious authority.
